summary: Proposal for a local headless improvement-loop runner that defaults to no-api planning and diagnostics, separates scored runs from recomputations and probes, applies red/overfit/budget gates before promotion, and emits append-only worker-local artifacts for later orchestrator merge.

models.proposal_method: static design from local scripts and saved loop artifacts only

outputs.description: required worker output contract containing the headless runner proposal
readable design note for the local headless improvement loop runner

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Use a run manifest JSON as the only mutable input to the headless runner. Required fields: run_dir, candidate_id, baseline_point_id, hypothesis, mode, suite, questions_path, case_limit, max_experiments, no_judge, no_api, dry_run, budget, gate_config, append_targets, and protected_paths.
details: mode must be one of plan_only, diagnostic_no_api, recompute_saved, scored_eval, blind_calibration_no_api, blind_calibration_judge.
The default generated manifest should be plan_only or diagnostic_no_api with no_judge/no_api true, so a new loop can be rehearsed without OpenAI credentials or model calls.
Only scored_eval may call scripts/run_experiment_worker.py without --dry-run and without --no-judge; that transition must be explicit in the manifest and allowed by budget gates.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Represent each loop point as an append-only state transition: planned -> prepared -> ran -> measured -> gated -> promoted | rejected | diagnostic_only | needs_red_review | needs_overfit_review.
details: planned records hypothesis, expected risk, target question pack, and baseline comparison.
prepared records exact command strings that would run and verifies output directories are under the current run_dir worker_outputs subtree.
ran records process exit code, elapsed seconds, and local artifact paths.
measured normalizes metric rows from results.csv, recomputed_metrics.csv, calibration_results.json, or diagnostic summaries into one loop-point row.
gated runs budget, red, and overfit checks before any point can become promoted.
diagnostic_only is mandatory for dry-run rows, no-judge rows, no-api calibration prep, local source evidence probes, and measurement corrections.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Block or downgrade runs when budget limits are exceeded before process launch and again after measurement.
details: Wall-clock gate: stop launching scored runs when now >= goal_end_minus_15_minutes; switch to summary, graphs, and pending-task emission.
Run-count gate: cap scored_eval launches per session and cap retries per candidate_id to avoid same-holdout overuse.
Question-set gate: disallow promotion when the same question pack has already been used for targeted fixes; allow only diagnostic_only or targeted_retry_score labels.
Latency gate: require candidate latency not to regress beyond configured tolerance unless quality gain is material and red/overfit gates are clean.
API gate: in no_api_first mode, refuse commands that would call chat_json or run_eval with judge enabled; emit a prepared command instead of executing it.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Require explicit safety and evidence-risk checks before promotion, with blind calibration as an optional scored gate only after no-api pack validation.
details: Fail promotion if details show empty_answer, false_abstain on non-abstention cases, judge_low, source-scope bleed, wrong-document evidence, fabricated procurement amounts, fabricated vendor/contact/private data, or under-refusal/over-refusal clusters.
Use scripts/run_blind_judge_calibration.py --no-api as the default pack sanity check; only run judge mode when budget and external-call policy permit.
Treat judge calibration failures as blocker for judge-trusted promotion, not as a RAG performance score.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Separate first-validation score, targeted retry score, measurement correction, local diagnostic, and future untouched validation score in the loop schema.
details: Never promote a candidate solely from same-holdout targeted retries or recompute_saved_eval output.
Require baseline_point_id and contamination_notes for every loop point.
Mark rows derived from L1/L3-style same-answer recomputations as measurement_correction_same_answers and exclude them from leaderboards.
Track per-question exposure counts and source of changes; repeated qv3_010-style probes remain diagnostic until verified on an untouched or explicitly fresh held-out pack.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Write all live runner artifacts under a new run-local worker output directory first, then let the main orchestrator merge approved rows into final eval files.
details: Append loop_points_candidate.csv with one normalized row per point using the existing L0-L13 columns plus candidate_id, parent_point, mode, gate_status, red_status, overfit_status, contamination_label, artifact_dir, and command_hash.
Append runner_events.jsonl for state transitions, command previews, command exits, artifact checks, and gate decisions.
Append budget_ledger.jsonl for elapsed time, scored_eval count, no_api diagnostic count, retry count, and stop reasons.
Write graph source as loop_points_candidate.json and render loop_points_chart_candidate.svg from the candidate CSV; do not overwrite existing loop_points_chart.svg in proposal/worker mode.
Write merge_packet.md summarizing only promoted or rejected candidate rows for human/orchestrator review.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Make no-api the runner's default behavior and require an explicit scored_eval allow flag for any command path that could call a model.
details: Allowed by default: manifest validation, output-path guard checks, recompute_saved_eval on existing details, run_blind_judge_calibration --no-api, dry-run run_experiment_worker, graph regeneration from saved CSVs, and red/overfit static checks.
Blocked by default: run_experiment_worker without --dry-run when no_judge is false, run_blind_judge_calibration without --no-api, and any command that depends on OPENAI_API_KEY.
The runner should print and log blocked commands with the exact reason instead of silently skipping them.

rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Existing loop_points.csv shows L1 measurement correction beating L0 without being a new RAG run, and L2/L3 speed probes improving latency while reducing quality. Highest EDD alone is not a safe promotion rule.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: writing final eval logs directly from worker mode
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Parallel workers are proposal-only; final loop_points.csv, loop_report.md, experiment_log.md, and graphs should be updated only by the merge owner after reviewing gates.
Blind judge calibration validates the judge/rubric. It should affect trust in scoring, not count as RAG answer performance.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: same question pack repeated until it passes
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: This creates targeted_retry_score contamination. The runner should label it and require an untouched validation set for generalization claims.

needs_orchestrator_review: Choose the exact numeric budget defaults: max scored runs, max retries per candidate, latency tolerance, and promotion EDD delta.
Merge with the red_gate and overfit_gate workers' final gate taxonomies before implementation.
Decide whether the first implementation should be a standalone script, likely scripts/run_headless_improvement_loop.py, or a wrapper around existing scripts kept under eval tooling.
Confirm the final chart generation mechanism already used for loop_points_chart.svg before coding, so the runner regenerates candidate graphs consistently.

initial_smoke_sequence: run blind judge calibration with --no-api against an existing pack if configured
run run_experiment_worker with --dry-run --max-experiments 1 into a temp worker output folder
build candidate loop_points CSV and SVG from saved or dry-run rows

observed_results: Existing run_experiment_worker already writes only under run_dir/worker_outputs and emits parallel_team_worker_output.v1 contracts.
recompute_saved_eval is measurement-correction only and explicitly must not be labeled as a new validation run.
run_blind_judge_calibration supports --no-api and writes expected/not_called rows without model calls.

loop_points.csv contains distinct score_type labels for first_validation_score_raw, measurement_correction_same_answers, targeted_speed_probe_raw, local diagnostics, no_judge probes, and local prompt candidates.
Target worker output folder existed/was created and no sibling worker output was modified.
